texto: I really like to cook. I used to do it lots for Mom, who's almost as useless as you, and it means I can practice describing all the different dishes. That always makes narratives seem less repetitive - and you can use them as metaphors, too.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephfordy Mayo: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “I really like to cook. I used to do it lots for Mom, who's almost as useless as you, and it means…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, food, parody pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humor e parody.

frase_original: I really like to cook. I used to do it lots for Mom, who's almost as useless as you, and it means I can practice describing all the different dishes. That always makes narratives seem less repetitive - and you can use them as metaphors, too.

texto: I realized with horror that I'd left my thesaurus in English class, and so wouldn't be able to describe their beauty in suitably poetic terms, but let me tell you, they were smokin' hot and no bullshit.
explicacao: Citação de Stephfordy Mayo sobre temas ligados a humor. “I realized with horror that I'd left my thesaurus in English class, and so wouldn't be able to de…” articula humor, beauty, parody em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como humor e parody.
